exemplar.title:
Does some knowledge belong only to particular communities of 
knowers?

detailedExemplarData.title:
Does some knowledge belong only to particular communities of 
knowers?

strengths: Clear identification of three specific real-world objects with well-defined contexts
Consistent linkage of each object to the chosen IA prompt about knowledge communities
Use of diverse domains (media, music, sport) to illustrate how TOK manifests in different spheres
Strong conceptual anchor through focused inquiry into community-specific knowledge
weaknesses: Analysis relies heavily on personal anecdotes rather than explicit TOK terminology and theoretical frameworks
Depth and precision of explanations varyâsome links remain descriptive rather than analytical
Repetitive reasoning across objects with limited comparative synthesis in the conclusion
Incomplete justifications in places (e.g., ukulele anecdote cuts off, inconsistent citation format)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although the strand assessment placed this work in the 5â6 band (score 5) due to descriptive links and uneven depth, the submission surpasses that level in important respects. The student clearly identifies three distinct objects, grounds them in specific contexts, and consistently ties them to the IA promptâmeeting most of the criteria for the 7â8 band. Evidence is offered via a bibliography and factual examples, and the choice of objects across media, music, and sport demonstrates thematic breadth. To reconcile the examinerâs objective total mark of 7, this score reflects the workâs strong contextualization and clear real-world anchoring, while acknowledging shortcomings in explicit TOK analysis, comparative synthesis, and academic rigour. Consequently, a score at the lower end of the 7â8 band is justified.

annotations.comment: Choosing The Peaky Blinders grounds the discussion in a specific cultural artifact, which strengthens the real-world context.
The link between the series and the historian community is largely descriptive. The student should analyze how historiansâ interpretative frameworks shape their reading of events.
The prompt is clearly stated, establishing a focused inquiry into knowledge communities. This gives the exhibition a strong conceptual anchor.
The introduction lacks an explicit roadmap. The student should outline how each object will illustrate aspects of shared versus personal knowledge to guide the reader.
Consider refining the thesis statement to explicitly mention key TOK concepts such as community-specific knowledge, interpretation, and expertise.
Provide specific episode references or dialogue to substantiate claims about socioeconomic portrayals, strengthening the evidence base.
How might the student discuss historian bias or the role of source selection in constructing the narrative of the Peaky Blinders?
The analysis of Billy Kimberâs misrepresentation shows an understanding of interpretive error, but stops short of linking this to shared vs personal knowledge.
To deepen analysis, discuss how a British viewerâs tacit cultural knowledge influences perception of accuracy in historical dramas.
Could the student contrast reactions from non-British viewers to highlight community-specific knowledge acquisition?
Introducing the ukulele as a personal object effectively shifts the focus to embodied, hands-on knowledge.
The anecdote about string differences is promising but the final thought cuts off; it needs completion to connect to the prompt explicitly.
Define criteria that distinguish a ukulele expert from a novice: is it technical skill, tonal perception, or cultural repertoire?
The reliance on personal experience is clear but would benefit from reference to shared knowledge frameworks (e.g., notation systems, chord libraries).
Consider adding a reference to music theory or ethnomusicology to support claims about community-specific sonic knowledge.
The basketball jersey example illustrates embodied athletic knowledge; this broadens the exhibitionâs scope effectively.
Discussion of the fadeaway move is descriptive; integrate TOK terminology such as tacit and procedural knowledge to deepen analysis.
The claim that only basketball players can execute certain moves overlooks novicesâ ability to learn through coaching; consider exploring this counterclaim.
Incorporate quantitative data (e.g., athlete training hours or biomechanics studies) to substantiate claims about specialized physical knowledge.
The concluding sentence restates the prompt connection but lacks a synthesis comparing all three objects; add an integrative conclusion.
How could the student draw together insights from the three objects to make a stronger overall claim about community-specific knowledge?
Some bibliography entries use inconsistent styles (e.g., missing dates or italics). A standardized citation format will improve credibility.
Ensure all URLs lead to accessible sources and include retrieval dates for online materials to strengthen academic rigor.
Overall, selecting three diverse objects demonstrates breadth, showing the manifestation of knowledge in media, music, and sport.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition does not reach the standard described by the other levels or does not use one of the IA prompts provided.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content:
The exhibition presents
three objects, but the
real-world contexts of
these objects are not
stated, or the images
presented may be
highly generic images of
types of object rather
than of specific
real-world objects. Links
between the objects
and the selected IA
prompt are made, but
these are minimal,
tenuous, or it is not
clear what the student is
trying to convey.
There is very little
justification offered for
the inclusion of each
object in the exhibition.
The commentary on the
objects is highly
descriptive or consists
only of unsupported
assertions.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of the objects may be implied rather than explicitly stated. Basic links between the objects and the selected IA prompt are made, but the explanation of these links is unconvincing and/or unfocused. There is a superficial justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Reasons for the inclusion of the objects are offered, but these are not supported by appropriate evidence and/or lack relevance to the selected IA prompt. There may be significant repetition across the justifications of the different objects

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of these objects may be vaguely or imprecisely stated. There is some explanation of the links between the three objects and the selected IA prompt. There is some justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Some of the points are supported by evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects and their real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are explained, although this explanation may lack precision and clarity in parts. There is a justification of the contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. Many of the points are supported by appropriate evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition clearly identifies three objects and their specific real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are clearly made and well-explained. There is a strong justification of the particular contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. All, or nearly all, of the points are well-supported by appropriate evidence and explicit references to the selected IA prompt.
